revision.text:
[[en:Hibasu-hime]][[fa:هیباسوهیمه نو میکوتو]][[ja:日葉酢媛命]][[zh:日叶酢媛命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|1=Empress of Japan}}|spouse={{ill|Suinin|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}|children={{ill|Keikō|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}; {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|qid=Q11372194|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko}}; {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qid=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}|father={{ill|Tanba no Michinushi|qid=Q11368587|1=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto}}|mother={{ill|Q109930105|qid=Q109930105|1=Tanba no Kawakami no Masurōjo}}}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Hibasu-hime no Mikoto | consort_reason = Empress of the 11th Emperor | image = | image_size = | caption = | alternative_name = | reign_period = | Japanese_reign_period = | Empress_date = 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}, 8th month, 1st day | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = 32nd year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}, 7th month, 6th day | death_place = | burial_place = | posthumous_name = | other_titles = Hibasune no Mikoto, Hibasuhime no Mikoto (''Kojiki'') | clan = | father = [[Tanba no Michinushi no Miko]] | mother = Tanba no Kawakami no Masurowame | spouse = {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} | entered_palace = 15th year of Emperor Suinin, 2nd month, 10th day | children = [[Itonoshiiri no Mikoto]]
{{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}
Ōnakahime no Mikoto
[[Yamatohime no Mikoto]]
Wakakii no Mikoto | foster_children = | palace = | empty_field_title1 = alternate_name | empty_field1 = Hiwasuhime no Mikoto, Hibasuhime no Mikoto | notes = }} '''Hibasu-hime no Mikoto''' (日葉酢媛命, birth date unknown – 32nd year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}, 7th month, 6th day) was the second {{ill|Empress|en|Empress|qid=Q100387742|lt=Empress|1=Empress}} of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}. Hibasu-hime no Mikoto was the daughter of [[Tanba no Michinushi no Miko]] and Tanba no Kawakami no Masurowame. She bore {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} (the 12th emperor) and two other princes and princesses. Her name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as "Hibasu-hime" but is also rendered as "Hibasune no Mikoto" or "Hibasuhime no Mikoto." The ''[[Kojiki]]'' refers to her as "Hiwasuhime no Mikoto" or "Hibasuhime no Mikoto." According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' after the death of [[Saho-hime no Mikoto]], the first empress of Emperor Suinin, her dying wish was for Hibasu-hime to succeed her. Hibasu-hime was invited to the imperial court along with her sisters in the 15th year of Emperor Suinin’s reign (2nd month, 10th day). She was officially installed as empress in the 8th month of the same year. Hibasu-hime passed away in the 32nd year of Emperor Suinin’s reign. During her funeral, Emperor Suinin lamented the cruel custom of human sacrifice at burials and asked his ministers for alternatives. [[Nomino Sukune]] proposed substituting terracotta figurines ({{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}) in place of live humans. These were first used at her Kofun and became the norm for subsequent royal funerals. === Burial === Her tomb, designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}, is the '''Saki-no-Terama-no-Misasagi''' (狹木之寺間陵), located in [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. The burial site, known archaeologically as the [[Saki Misasagiyama Kofun|Saki Misasagi-yama Kofun]], is a keyhole-shaped tomb mound 207 meters in length. == References == {{reflist}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-hist-stub}} ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== '''Hibasu-hime''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{Consorts of Japan}}]] {{translated page|ja|日葉酢媛命|version=100274474|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Hibasu-hime]] [[ja:日葉酢媛命]] [[fa:%D9%87%DB%8C%D8%A8%D8%A7%D8%B3%D9%88%D9%87%DB%8C%D9%85%D9%87 %D9%86%D9%88 %D9%85%DB%8C%DA%A9%D9%88%D8%AA%D9%88]] [[fa:هیباسوهیمه نو میکوتو]] [[zh:日叶酢媛命]] {{wikidata link|Q8375821}} [[Category:Ancient Japanese women]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members before the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]
